authorPois.name: Biltema Gol
authorPois.description: Not a regular bike shop, but the do sell bike parts and bike tools and a lot of other stuff.

authorPois.name: Tent spot
authorPois.description: Nice place to pitch a tent.

authorPois.name: Coop Mega Røros
authorPois.description: Shopping center with groceries, drugstore etc...

authorPois.name: Høre Stavkyrkje
authorPois.description: Høre Stavkyrkje is a stave church built in 1179.

authorPois.name: Gjelten Bru Camping
authorPois.description: Camping with 13 cottages and places to tent.

authorPois.name: M/B Bitihorn
authorPois.description: It is possible to take this boat to Eidsbugarden, and them bike to Tyinkrysset, down to Øyo, and them bike along the lake Vangsmjøse and connect with the route. It is recommended to cycle along the north shore of Vangsmjøse, this is a gravel road with little trafic.

authorPois.name: Damaged road.
authorPois.description: The road Hinøglslivegen between Sikkilsdalsvegen and Jotunheimvegen was damaged by the storm Hans in august 2023, but it is still passable with a bike, but you may need to hike-a-bike a short section.

authorPois.name: Damaged road.
authorPois.description: The road Høgspenten between Nysetvegen and Sangefjellvegen was damaged by the storm Hans in august 2023, but it is still passable with a bike, but you may need to hike-a-bike several short sections.

authorPois.name: Jutulhogget
authorPois.description: Jutulhogget is a 2.4-kilometre (1.5 mi) long dry canyon in the municipalities of Alvdal and Rendalen in Innlandet county, Norway. The canyon was created by the rupture of the dam of a proglacial lake and the force of the rushing water leaving the lake carved out the canyon. The canyon was protected as a nature reserve in 1959. The steep walls of the canyon range from 100 to 240 metres (330 to 790 ft) in height.

authorPois.name: Riddarspranget
authorPois.description:
Ridderspranget is a ravine in the river Sjoa. It was formed by the river gnawing deep into the bedrock, so that erosion has only occurred in the riverbed.

According to the folklore collector Ivar Kleiven, it was the knight Sigvat at Kvie in Valdres who had kidnapped a beautiful girl, called Skårvangssolen, from the knight Ivar Gjesling at Sandbu in Vågå. Sigvat put Skårvangssolen on his horse and rode off. Ivar set off after Sigvat and caught up with him again at Ridderspranget. But the horse with Sigvat and Skårvangssolen on top jumped over the ravine with the gushing water below. From there, he prevented Ivar and his men from trying to make the jump after him.

name: Setrer og villmark (Shielings and wilderness) - Gravel from Røros to Flåm
